parent.label: Books
parent.description: Long-form human thought — the most densely argued, most carefully edited form of written knowledge. Books teach models sustained reasoning across hundreds of pages, narrative coherence, chapter structure, and the thousand-year accumulation of ideas that constitute Western (and increasingly global) intellectual heritage. The legal status of this category is contested.
bullets: Project Gutenberg: 70,000+ public-domain works — everything before 1928
Books3 / LibGen derivatives: vast collections of copyrighted books scraped without authorization
Long-context coherence: books are the primary training signal for multi-paragraph reasoning

cells.label: Public Domain
cells.description: Project Gutenberg, Standard Ebooks, and other collections of works in the public domain — everything published before copyright applied or that has since expired. Austen, Dickens, Darwin, Twain, Du Bois, Wollstonecraft.
bullets: Project Gutenberg: 70,000+ works, all pre-1928 in the US or author-donated
Standard Ebooks: typographically careful, machine-readable reformatting of Gutenberg texts
Historical prose — Victorian sentences, 18th-century rhetoric — shapes the model's sense of 'formal writing'
cells.screenshotCaption: Project Gutenberg — 70,000+ public domain books, freely available

cells.label: Copyrighted Books
cells.description: Books3, LibGen, and similar datasets derived from shadow libraries and pirated scans — millions of copyrighted books scraped and included without author consent. This is the most legally and ethically contested part of the training corpus, and the subject of ongoing litigation.
bullets: Books3 (The Pile): ~196,640 books scraped from Bibliotik, a private tracker
LibGen: ~4M books, used by several major training runs under contested 'fair use' arguments
Multiple lawsuits filed by authors (Authors Guild, individual writers) against AI companies
cells.screenshotCaption: Internet Archive book digitization — a public-domain adjacent source; Books3 used more opaque piracy channels
